title_th: พระราชบัญญัติการบริหารงานและการให้บริการภาครัฐผ่านระบบดิจิทัล พ.ศ. 2562
description: Establishes the framework for digital transformation of government services in Thailand. Mandates government agencies to provide services through digital channels, implement data sharing between agencies, and adopt open data principles. Creates the Government Data Committee to oversee data governance and interoperability across government systems. Promotes digital identity verification and electronic service delivery.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: Section 1. This Act shall be called the Government Digital Services Act, B.E. 2562 (2019).

provisions.title: Government Data Committee
provisions.content: Section 5. There shall be a Government Data Committee consisting of the Prime Minister or a Deputy Prime Minister designated by the Prime Minister as the chairperson, the Minister of Digital Economy and Society as vice-chairperson, and not more than nine qualified members appointed by the Cabinet. The Committee shall have the duties and powers to establish policies, strategies, and standards for government data management and digital services.

provisions.title: Digital service delivery obligation
provisions.content: Section 10. Government agencies shall provide services to the public through digital systems and shall not require the public to submit documents or evidence that are already in the possession of or accessible to the government agency or any other government agency through digital systems, unless such information is not accessible through digital systems.

provisions.title: Government data sharing
provisions.content: Section 14. Government agencies shall share data with other government agencies through digital systems in accordance with the standards and procedures prescribed by the Government Data Committee, subject to the laws on personal data protection and official information. Government agencies shall not refuse to share data without reasonable justification.

provisions.title: Open government data
provisions.content: Section 20. Government agencies shall make government data available to the public in open data format through digital systems, subject to the restrictions prescribed by law on national security, personal data protection, or official secrets.
